title: Robust Hybrid Successive-Substitution-Newton Methods for the Isothermal Flash Problem in Multicomponent Natural Gas Systems
novelty_statement: Adaptive SS-to-NR switching criterion based on convergence rate monitoring, combined with trust-region Newton step control, improves flash robustness for natural gas mixtures near the critical point. Systematic open-source benchmark with full reproducibility.

research_questions.question: Does an adaptive SS-NR switching criterion improve convergence robustness compared to a fixed-tolerance switch?
research_questions.hypothesis: Monitoring the convergence rate (ratio of successive residuals) detects stalling earlier and triggers NR before SS diverges, reducing failures by >50%.
research_questions.method: Paired comparison of baseline vs candidate on 1500+ flash cases across 6 fluid families, using Wilcoxon signed-rank test.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Statistically significant improvement (p < 0.05) in convergence rate, with Cliff's delta > 0.33 (medium effect).

research_questions.question: Does a trust-region safeguard on the Newton step prevent divergence near the mixture critical point?
research_questions.hypothesis: Clipping the Newton step to a trust-region radius prevents overshooting when the Jacobian is ill-conditioned near the critical locus.
research_questions.method: Targeted benchmark of 50+ near-critical cases with condition number tracking.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Convergence rate for near-critical cases improves from <80% to >95%.

research_questions.question: What is the computational overhead of the proposed modifications?
research_questions.hypothesis: The overhead is negligible (<5% increase in median CPU time) because the modifications affect only the switching logic and step control, not the core EOS evaluation.
research_questions.method: Timing comparison with 3 repeated trials per case, reported as median +/- IQR.

claims_to_validate.text: The candidate algorithm achieves higher convergence rate than baseline across all fluid families
Median iteration count is reduced by the candidate algorithm
CPU time overhead of the candidate is less than 10%
Near-critical convergence rate improves from <80% to >95%
